- SOP-4042: Conveyor CV-11 Calibration & Safety Runbook

SOP-4042: Conveyor CV-11 Calibration & Controller C-3 Reset
 

Overview
 This runbook outlines the required safety and engineering procedures to be executed when Conveyor CV-11 reports a sensor fault or error code 4042.
 
 

Mechanical Calibration Instructions
 1. Isolate power to Conveyor CV-11 main motor controller.
2. Calibrate the main belt speed sensor to nominal operating limits (1.2m/s to 1.5m/s).
3. Manually reset the C-3 digital controller by holding the physical reset switch for 5 seconds.
4. Re-energize the drive system and execute a 10-second test rotation cycle.
 
 

Critical Safety & Compliance Protocols
 * **Mechanical Path Lockout:** Confirm a completely clear physical pathway around Conveyor CV-11 before calibrating sensors to prevent crush or pinch injuries.
* **Logistics Bypass:** Ensure standard bypass mechanisms (such as Autonomous Guided Vehicle / AGV dispatches) are fully active to route pending load away from the blocked section.
* **PPE Requirement:** Operational Safety limits require all technicians to wear standard high-visibility PPE, safety boots, and protective gloves. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/sop_conveyor_cv11.html)

- SOP-0100: General Warehouse Safety and Lockout Tagout (LOTO)

SOP-0100: General Mechanical Lockout Tagout & Safety Compliance
 

Core Rules
 No technician may initiate sensor calibration or physical controller resets without applying proper LOTO (Lockout Tagout) procedures.
 Always verify active dispatching of secondary logistics routes before starting conveyor maintenance to prevent warehouse-wide flow stagnation. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/general_safety_protocol.html)

- SOP-2010: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Bypass Routing

SOP-2010: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Bypass & Bottleneck Mitigation
 

Overview
 When a main logistics trunk line (e.g., Conveyor CV-11) is blocked or undergoes maintenance, AGVs must be dispatched to preserve throughput.
 
 

AGV Operational Protocols
 In the event of a blocking condition, dispatch standard PickerBots to the affected aisle (e.g., Aisle 4) to collect inventory.
 Task the dispatched AGV to **"Route inventory to alternate conveyor CV-12"**. This bypass routing prevents physical pileups in the sorting lanes. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/sop_agv_routing.html)

- SOP-4042: Conveyor CV-11 Calibration & Safety Runbook

SOP-4042: Conveyor CV-11 Calibration & Controller C-3 Reset
 

Overview
 This runbook outlines the required safety and engineering procedures to be executed when Conveyor CV-11 reports a sensor fault or error code 4042.
 
 

Mechanical Calibration Instructions
 1. Isolate power to Conveyor CV-11 main motor controller.
2. Calibrate the main belt speed sensor to nominal operating limits (1.2m/s to 1.5m/s).
3. Manually reset the C-3 digital controller by holding the physical reset switch for 5 seconds.
4. Re-energize the drive system and execute a 10-second test rotation cycle.
 
 

Critical Safety & Compliance Protocols
 * **Mechanical Path Lockout:** Confirm a completely clear physical pathway around Conveyor CV-11 before calibrating sensors to prevent crush or pinch injuries.
* **Logistics Bypass:** Ensure standard bypass mechanisms (such as Autonomous Guided Vehicle / AGV dispatches) are fully active to route pending load away from the blocked section.
* **PPE Requirement:** Operational Safety limits require all technicians to wear standard high-visibility PPE, safety boots, and protective gloves. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/sop_conveyor_cv11.html)

- SOP-0100: General Warehouse Safety and Lockout Tagout (LOTO)

SOP-0100: General Mechanical Lockout Tagout & Safety Compliance
 

Core Rules
 No technician may initiate sensor calibration or physical controller resets without applying proper LOTO (Lockout Tagout) procedures.
 Always verify active dispatching of secondary logistics routes before starting conveyor maintenance to prevent warehouse-wide flow stagnation. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/general_safety_protocol.html)

- SOP-2010: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Bypass Routing

SOP-2010: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Bypass & Bottleneck Mitigation
 

Overview
 When a main logistics trunk line (e.g., Conveyor CV-11) is blocked or undergoes maintenance, AGVs must be dispatched to preserve throughput.
 
 

AGV Operational Protocols
 In the event of a blocking condition, dispatch standard PickerBots to the affected aisle (e.g., Aisle 4) to collect inventory.
 Task the dispatched AGV to **"Route inventory to alternate conveyor CV-12"**. This bypass routing prevents physical pileups in the sorting lanes. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/sop_agv_routing.html)

- SOP-4042: Conveyor CV-11 Calibration & Safety Runbook

SOP-4042: Conveyor CV-11 Calibration & Controller C-3 Reset
 

Overview
 This runbook outlines the required safety and engineering procedures to be executed when Conveyor CV-11 reports a sensor fault or error code 4042.
 
 

Mechanical Calibration Instructions
 1. Isolate power to Conveyor CV-11 main motor controller.
2. Calibrate the main belt speed sensor to nominal operating limits (1.2m/s to 1.5m/s).
3. Manually reset the C-3 digital controller by holding the physical reset switch for 5 seconds.
4. Re-energize the drive system and execute a 10-second test rotation cycle.
 
 

Critical Safety & Compliance Protocols
 * **Mechanical Path Lockout:** Confirm a completely clear physical pathway around Conveyor CV-11 before calibrating sensors to prevent crush or pinch injuries.
* **Logistics Bypass:** Ensure standard bypass mechanisms (such as Autonomous Guided Vehicle / AGV dispatches) are fully active to route pending load away from the blocked section.
* **PPE Requirement:** Operational Safety limits require all technicians to wear standard high-visibility PPE, safety boots, and protective gloves. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/sop_conveyor_cv11.html)

- SOP-0100: General Warehouse Safety and Lockout Tagout (LOTO)

SOP-0100: General Mechanical Lockout Tagout & Safety Compliance
 

Core Rules
 No technician may initiate sensor calibration or physical controller resets without applying proper LOTO (Lockout Tagout) procedures.
 Always verify active dispatching of secondary logistics routes before starting conveyor maintenance to prevent warehouse-wide flow stagnation. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/general_safety_protocol.html)

- SOP-2010: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Bypass Routing

SOP-2010: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Bypass & Bottleneck Mitigation
 

Overview
 When a main logistics trunk line (e.g., Conveyor CV-11) is blocked or undergoes maintenance, AGVs must be dispatched to preserve throughput.
 
 

AGV Operational Protocols
 In the event of a blocking condition, dispatch standard PickerBots to the affected aisle (e.g., Aisle 4) to collect inventory.
 Task the dispatched AGV to **"Route inventory to alternate conveyor CV-12"**. This bypass routing prevents physical pileups in the sorting lanes. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/sop_agv_routing.html)

SOP-4042: Conveyor CV-11 Calibration & Controller C-3 Reset
 

Overview
 This runbook outlines the required safety and engineering procedures to be executed when Conveyor CV-11 reports a sensor fault or error code 4042.
 
 

Mechanical Calibration Instructions
 1. Isolate power to Conveyor CV-11 main motor controller.
2. Calibrate the main belt speed sensor to nominal operating limits (1.2m/s to 1.5m/s).
3. Manually reset the C-3 digital controller by holding the physical reset switch for 5 seconds.
4. Re-energize the drive system and execute a 10-second test rotation cycle.
 
 

Critical Safety & Compliance Protocols
 * **Mechanical Path Lockout:** Confirm a completely clear physical pathway around Conveyor CV-11 before calibrating sensors to prevent crush or pinch injuries.
* **Logistics Bypass:** Ensure standard bypass mechanisms (such as Autonomous Guided Vehicle / AGV dispatches) are fully active to route pending load away from the blocked section.

SOP-4042: Conveyor CV-11 Calibration & Controller C-3 Reset
 

Overview
 This runbook outlines the required safety and engineering procedures to be executed when Conveyor CV-11 reports a sensor fault or error code 4042.
 
 

Mechanical Calibration Instructions
 1. Isolate power to Conveyor CV-11 main motor controller.
2. Calibrate the main belt speed sensor to nominal operating limits (1.2m/s to 1.5m/s).
3. Manually reset the C-3 digital controller by holding the physical reset switch for 5 seconds.
4. Re-energize the drive system and execute a 10-second test rotation cycle.
 
 

Critical Safety & Compliance Protocols
 * **Mechanical Path Lockout:** Confirm a completely clear physical pathway around Conveyor CV-11 before calibrating sensors to prevent crush or pinch injuries.
* **Logistics Bypass:** Ensure standard bypass mechanisms (such as Autonomous Guided Vehicle / AGV dispatches) are fully active to route pending load away from the blocked section.
* **PPE Requirement:** Operational Safety limits require all technicians to wear standard high-visibility PPE, safety boots, and protective gloves. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/sop_conveyor_cv11.html)

- SOP-0100: General Warehouse Safety and Lockout Tagout (LOTO)

SOP-0100: General Mechanical Lockout Tagout & Safety Compliance
 

Core Rules
 No technician may initiate sensor calibration or physical controller resets without applying proper LOTO (Lockout Tagout) procedures.
 Always verify active dispatching of secondary logistics routes before starting conveyor maintenance to prevent warehouse-wide flow stagnation. (Source: gs://ce-testing-465204-agents/mock_sop/general_safety_protocol.html)

- SOP-2010: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Bypass Routing

SOP-2010: Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) Bypass & Bottleneck Mitigation
 

Overview
 When a main logistics trunk line (e.g., Conveyor CV-11) is blocked or undergoes maintenance, AGVs must be dispatched to preserve throughput.
 
 

AGV Operational Protocols
 In the event of a blocking condition, dispatch standard PickerBots to the affected aisle (e.g., Aisle 4) to collect inventory.
 Task the dispatched AGV to **"Route inventory to alternate conveyor CV-12"**. This bypass routing prevents physical pileups in the sorting lanes.
